name: Mac Drag Scroll
description: Native-feeling middle-mouse drag scrolling for external mice on macOS.
homepage_url_justification: The project website links to the current repository, documentation, and releases: https://martincalander.com/macdragscroll
report_url_justification: Public bug reports and feature requests are accepted at https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/issues; private security reports follow SECURITY.md.
description_good_justification: README.md describes what Mac Drag Scroll does, who it is for, how to install it, how to grant permissions, and how to use it.
interact_justification: README.md links SUPPORT.md and GitHub issues. SUPPORT.md explains what information to include when opening an issue.
contribution_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents the contribution process, build/test commands, pull request expectations, documentation style, and release note expectations.
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md defines coding standards, local checks, pull request requirements, and when tests or documentation must change: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
code_of_conduct_justification: The project publishes expected behavior, unacceptable behavior, private reporting, and enforcement in a standard repository file: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
sites_https_justification: The project homepage, source repository, release downloads, and update metadata are hosted on HTTPS GitHub URLs.
floss_license_justification: The repository has an MIT LICENSE file.
floss_license_osi_justification: MIT is an OSI-approved open source license.
license_location_justification: The license is in the top-level LICENSE file: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/LICENSE
documentation_basics_justification: README.md documents installation, permissions, use, settings, diagnostics, privacy, updates, support, and requirements.
documentation_interface_justification: README.md documents the user-facing interface and settings; CONTRIBUTING.md documents the developer-facing build and test interface.
documentation_roadmap_justification: ROADMAP.md defines priorities, intended work through July 2027, collaboration milestones, and explicit non-goals: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/ROADMAP.md
documentation_architecture_justification: ARCHITECTURE.md documents the major components, input flow, safety invariants, storage, distribution, and verification boundaries: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md
documentation_security_justification: SECURITY.md documents local-data, permission, input-safety, update-verification, unsigned-distribution, reporting, and response expectations: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/SECURITY.md
documentation_quick_start_justification: README.md provides short Homebrew, direct-download, and CLI installation paths followed by permission and usage steps: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll#install
documentation_current_justification: README.md, CHANGELOG.md, SUPPORT.md, PRIVACY.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and docs/RELEASING.md describe the current release behavior and distribution flow.
documentation_achievements_justification: The repository front page displays and links the OpenSSF Best Practices and Scorecard badges: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll#readme
repo_public_justification: The source repository is public on GitHub: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll
repo_track_justification: The project uses git history on GitHub to track changes, authors, and timestamps.
repo_interim_justification: Interim development changes are committed to git and reviewed through pull requests before merging to main.
repo_distributed_justification: The project uses git, a distributed version control system.
version_unique_justification: Public releases use unique SemVer tags plus a monotonically increasing CFBundleVersion build number.
version_semver_justification: docs/RELEASING.md requires SemVer for public releases.
version_tags_justification: Public releases are tagged in git with vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH tags.
release_notes_justification: CHANGELOG.md tracks notable user-facing changes by release.
release_notes_vulns_justification: SECURITY.md describes the vulnerability reporting process. Confirmed public vulnerability fixes should be included in CHANGELOG.md; no public vulnerabilities are currently known.
report_tracker_justification: Bug reports and feature requests are tracked with GitHub Issues using issue templates.
report_process_justification: SUPPORT.md explains when to open an issue and what information to include.
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues provide a public archive of bug reports and feature requests.
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: SECURITY.md documents how to report suspected vulnerabilities privately.
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: SECURITY.md asks reporters not to open public vulnerability issues and directs them to private vulnerability reporting or the maintainer contact path.
test_justification: The macdragscrollTests target contains automated tests for scroll calculations and settings behavior.
test_invocation_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents the xcodebuild test command for the macdragscroll scheme.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: The Quality Gate GitHub Actions workflow runs xcodebuild tests on pushes and pull requests targeting main.
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md tells contributors to add or update tests when changing scroll math, settings persistence, permissions, or update behavior.
tests_are_added_justification: Project policy requires tests for reliability-sensitive behavior changes, and current tests cover scroll calculations and settings behavior.
tests_documented_added_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents when tests should be added or updated.
static_analysis_justification: The repository runs CodeQL for Swift and documents xcodebuild analyze in CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/RELEASING.md.
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: CodeQL uses the security-extended query suite for Swift analysis.
static_analysis_often_justification: The CodeQL workflow runs on pushes to main, pull requests to main, weekly schedule, and manual dispatch.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: The repository uses GitHub secret scanning with push protection plus Gitleaks on pushes, pull requests, and a weekly schedule; SECURITY.md instructs contributors not to commit private keys or secrets.
internationalization_justification: The app ships localized resources for 14 locales, and installation documentation is maintained in English, Japanese, and Simplified Chinese: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/tree/main/macdragscroll
sites_password_security_justification: The project-owned website and application do not accept or store external-user passwords; repository authentication is provided by GitHub.
maintenance_or_update_justification: The project publishes maintained GitHub releases and signed Sparkle updates, with release history tracked in CHANGELOG.md: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/releases
vulnerability_response_process_justifica: SECURITY.md defines private reporting, review and response expectations, responsible remediation, and disclosure for confirmed issues: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/SECURITY.md
coding_standards_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md requires the Swift API Design Guidelines, existing architecture boundaries, warning-free analysis, focused helpers, and tests for reliability-sensitive changes: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#coding-standards
external_dependencies_justification: Sparkle is the only application dependency and is pinned by version and revision in Package.resolved. Playwright is a documentation-only development dependency pinned by exact version and npm integrity hashes: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/tree/main/docs/assets/source
dependency_monitoring_justification: Dependabot checks Swift Package Manager, npm, and pinned GitHub Actions dependencies weekly. Pull requests also run GitHub Dependency Review and block moderate-or-higher known vulnerabilities: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/.github/dependabot.yml
updateable_reused_components_justificati: Sparkle is managed through Swift Package Manager and monitored by Dependabot instead of being copied into the repository: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/.github/dependabot.yml
interfaces_current_justification: The project targets the current macOS/Xcode toolchain, uses supported AppKit and SwiftUI interfaces, and keeps Sparkle pinned to a current reviewed release.
input_validation_justification: Trigger configuration, input-source classification, preference decoding, numeric ranges, finite values, and crash-report filenames are validated before use and covered by tests: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/macdragscrollTests/SettingsManagerTests.swift
signed_releases_justification: Every public release ZIP includes a detached Sparkle EdDSA signature, and current releases publish GitHub build provenance for offline verification: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/releases
warnings_strict_justification: The required Quality Gate compiles tests, static analysis, and universal release builds with GCC_TREAT_WARNINGS_AS_ERRORS and SWIFT_TREAT_WARNINGS_AS_ERRORS enabled: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/.github/workflows/ci.yml
dynamic_analysis_justification: Every pull request and main-branch push runs the full XCTest suite under Xcode Address Sanitizer in the required Quality Gate: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/.github/workflows/ci.yml
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: Address Sanitizer runs against the Debug test configuration, where Swift and project assertions remain enabled.
static_analysis_fixed_justification: CodeQL and Xcode static analysis are required to complete without findings that fail their checks; compiler warnings are errors and suppressions require documented justification.
governance_justification: GOVERNANCE.md documents roles, change decisions, security and release ownership, and role changes: https://github.com/martincalander/MacDragScroll/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md
roles_responsibilities_justification: GOVERNANCE.md identifies the project lead, release owner, maintainers, contributors, and the responsibilities of each role.
code_review_standards_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md and GOVERNANCE.md define review criteria. The protected main ruleset enforces code-owner review, latest-push approval, resolved conversations, and required checks.
two_person_review_justification: The protected main ruleset requires two independent approvals for every pull request, including code-owner review, and does not allow bypass actors.
installation_common_justification: Users can install through a standard Homebrew cask or a drag-to-Applications DMG, both documented in README.md.
general_comments: These are evidence-backed proposals for maintainer review in BadgeApp. Access continuity and bus-factor criteria remain intentionally unclaimed while release signing and private security response still depend on the project lead.
